title: Alsophila hieronymi
intro: Alsophila hieronymi is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive fronds and robust growth habit. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Māori medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Alsophila hieronymi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
